features.summary: Goldman Sachs reported strong Q2 2021 results with net revenues of $15.4 billion (second highest on record) and earnings per share of $15.02, contributing to record first-half revenues of $33 billion. The firm demonstrated strength across all four business segments, with Investment Banking producing $3.6 billion in revenues, Global Markets generating $4.9 billion, Asset Management achieving record revenues of $5.1 billion, and Consumer & Wealth Management reaching record revenues of $1.7 billion. The board declared a 60% dividend increase to $2 per share, reflecting confidence in earnings durability.
features.management_outlook: Management remains optimistic about the opportunity set, citing record Investment Banking backlog levels and elevated CEO confidence driving strategic discussions. They expect continued progress on strategic priorities including expanding Transaction Banking internationally, growing the consumer platform, and transitioning Asset Management to more fee-based third-party funds. The firm is confident in achieving sustainable mid-teens returns and expects loan growth to accelerate in coming quarters across wealth management, investment banking, and mortgage warehouse lending.
features.risks: Key risks include potential pandemic resurgence from the Delta variant which could slow economic growth, geopolitical tensions particularly with China, and cybersecurity threats. The firm faces regulatory uncertainty from the Biden administration's Executive Order on competition and antitrust enforcement, which could impact M&A activity. Additionally, Goldman's stress capital buffer of 6.4% was higher than expected, making capital efficiency targets more challenging to achieve in the near term.
features.key_drivers: Performance was driven by strong client engagement across all businesses, market share gains in Global Markets, elevated M&A activity supported by post-pandemic strategic repositioning, and robust equity underwriting amid high IPO volumes. Asset Management benefited from a supportive market environment generating $3.7 billion in equity investment gains, while the firm continued harvesting on-balance sheet investments with $5.5 billion in dispositions year-to-date. Transaction Banking showed strong momentum with nearly 300 clients and $40 billion in deposits.
